dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9704 IH: Tariff Impacted Farmer Support Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Figures (for himself and Mr. Thompson of Mississippi) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make economic assistance payments to qualifying producers for certain revenue losses with respect to covered commodities, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Tariff Impacted Farmer Support Act of 2026.

section.header: Economic assistance payments to qualifying producers

subsection.text: Subject to subsection (c), for each of crop years 2025 and 2026, the Secretary of Agriculture shall make economic assistance payments to qualifying producers for revenue losses with respect to covered commodities.

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall make payments to each qualifying producer under subsection (a) using a formula determined by the Secretary in accordance with paragraph (2).
The formula determined under paragraph (1) shall—

subparagraph.text: with respect to a crop year, be based on the difference between—

clause.text: the total revenue of a qualifying producer with respect to covered commodities for the preceding crop year; minus
the total revenue of such qualifying producer with respect to covered commodities for that crop year; and

subparagraph.text: ensure that the total amount of payments made to qualifying producers under subsection (a) does not exceed the amount of funding specified in subsection (d).

subsection.text: A qualifying producer that receives an economic assistance payment under subsection (a) for revenue losses with respect to covered commodities for crop year 2025 may not receive a payment under such subsection for revenue losses with respect to covered commodities for crop year 2026.

paragraph.text: Of the funds of the Commodity Credit Corporation, the Secretary shall use $15,000,000,000 to carry out this section in accordance with paragraph (2).
From the amount of funding specified in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall use to make payments under subsection (a)—
The Secretary shall make payments under subsection (a)—

subparagraph.text: not later than November 1, 2026, for crop year 2025; and
not later than November 1, 2027, for crop year 2026.

paragraph.text: The term average adjusted gross income means the average adjusted gross income (as defined in section 1001D of the Food Security Act of 1985 (7 U.S.C. 1308–3a)) of a person or legal entity (as defined in section 1001 of such Act (7 U.S.C. 1308)) that derives from covered commodities.
The term covered commodity means corn, cotton, peanuts, poultry, or soybeans.
The term qualifying producer means a producer that—

subparagraph.text: has an average adjusted gross income of not more than $500,000 for—

clause.text: the crop year for which such producer seeks a payment under subsection (a); and
the crop year preceding such crop year; and

subparagraph.text: submits to the Secretary documentation of total revenue for each crop year described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A), including receipts, contracts, and any such other records as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

paragraph.text: The term total revenue means, with respect to a crop year, the amount equal to—

subparagraph.text: the average adjusted gross income of a qualifying producer for such crop year; minus
the cost of production relating to the covered commodities of such qualifying producer for such crop year.
